title: Domain-Agnostic Team Abstraction (Chattable-Team Phase 4)
description: Make a 'team' a data object rather than hardcoded pipeline logic, so adding a team is configuration not code. The existing programming pipeline becomes the first Team instance; the lessons memory store is namespaced per team; and the pipeline shape (encoded SOP) is resolved from the active team. This is the domain-agnostic spine that later domains (e.g. the deferred medical team) plug into. CLI-first, provider-agnostic, no new heavy deps. Note: this is the user's 'Phase 2' and corresponds to research roadmap Phase 4; the interrupt/approve/steer phase (research Phase 2) is intentionally not built here.

resolvedClarifications.answer: Plan research-roadmap Phase 4 'Team abstraction generalization' (the user's 'Phase 2'), NOT the interrupt/approve/steer phase (research Phase 2). Confirmed by the user against the memory note's mental model.

Option A: team definitions live as an optional `teams` section in bober.config.json plus a built-in 'programming' team registered in code. A `.bober/teams/*.json` file registry is explicitly deferred (can be layered on later by merging file teams with config teams without changing the Team type).

User selected 'supervised by default' and 'configurable per spawn' — but these pertain to the interrupt/approve/steer phase (research Phase 2), which is NOT built in this spec. Recorded for the future interrupt/steer plan; no effect on the Team abstraction.

Plan directly now (no bober-architect pass). Phase 4 reuses the existing role/provider routing, pipeline selector, and memory store; the new design (Team data model, per-team namespacing, team-aware shape selection) mirrors established Phase 1 patterns.
lets go with A for now write the sprints

assumptions: Scope is research-roadmap Phase 4 'Team abstraction generalization' (the user's 'Phase 2'). The interrupt/approve/steer-in-chat phase (research Phase 2) is OUT of scope here.

Phase 3 is not built, so the `semantic_facts` SQLite table does not exist yet. Per-team namespacing in this plan covers ONLY the existing `.bober/memory/` lessons store (src/state/memory.ts:15-25). `semantic_facts.scope` namespacing is deferred to Phase 3.

A team differs along three already-existing axes: per-role provider routing (RoleProviderMap via resolveRoleProviders, src/config/role-providers.ts:97), memory namespace, and pipeline shape (the encoded SOP resolved by selectPipelineEngine, src/orchestrator/workflow/selector.ts:50). No dynamic arbitrary-role execution graph is built; a different team's behavior is realized as a different pipeline shape ('same engine, different SOP').
Team definitions live as an optional `teams` record in bober.config.json plus a built-in 'programming' team registered in code (Option A). A `.bober/teams/*.json` file registry is deferred.
The default/programming team keeps the current `.bober/memory/` path (no migration; existing lessons stay live). Additional named teams get a `.bober/memory/<namespace>/` subdir.
The abstraction is validated with a minimal NON-medical example team defined purely as data (e.g. a 'docs'/'research-only' team). The medical team is not built.

Medical team, code-enforced guardrail enforcement, and the full medical SOP are OUT of scope (research Phase 6). The Team type may carry a forward-compat optional `guardrails` field, but nothing enforces it.

Zero behavior change for the existing programming pipeline is a hard requirement: the full existing test suite stays green and build+typecheck stay clean. loadTeam(config) with no team id must reproduce today's routing, shape, and memory path exactly.

CLI-first and provider-agnostic: everything runs identically on Anthropic and OpenAI-compatible (DeepSeek) providers. No new heavy dependencies; new code is ESM/NodeNext strict TS with .js import extensions, no SDK types leaking outside src/providers/.
outOfScope: The interrupt/approve/steer-in-chat phase: input-required/paused RunState, mid-run pause/resume, approve/reject/steer via chat (research Phase 2).
The semantic_facts SQLite table, bi-temporal facts, reconcile-on-write, and memory hygiene/decay (research Phase 3).
The medical team, code-enforced guardrails, health ingestion adapters, the code-as-tool sandbox, and literature retrieval (research Phase 6).
Dynamic arbitrary-role execution graphs, a visual team builder, and any team DSL beyond JSON config.
A `.bober/teams/*.json` file registry (deferred; Option A only for now).
Self-improvement loops (ExpeL fail->pass extraction, GEPA prompt evolution).
Any second PRODUCTION team beyond the minimal example fixture used to validate the abstraction.
Changing the public behavior of runPipeline, resolveRoleProviders, selectPipelineEngine, or the memory store for the default/programming team.

features.title: Team data model + registry + programming team as instance #1
features.description: A `Team` data type and a `loadTeam(config, teamId?)` resolver, with a built-in 'programming' team that reproduces today's behavior. An optional `teams` config section and `defaultTeam` field let additional teams be declared as data.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When loadTeam(config) is called with no team id, it returns the built-in 'programming' team whose providers deep-equal resolveRoleProviders(config), whose pipelineShape equals the resolved engine for config, and whose memoryNamespace maps to the current .bober/memory/ path.

AC2: When bober.config.json declares a `teams` entry and `defaultTeam`, the schema parses it and loadTeam(config, '<id>') returns a Team built from that entry (providers merged over resolved defaults, namespace and pipelineShape from the entry).
AC3: When loadTeam is called with an unknown team id, it throws a clear error naming the missing id.
AC4: The existing pipeline and chat continue to behave identically (no regression) because the active team defaults to 'programming' everywhere.

features.title: Per-team memory namespacing of the lessons store
features.description: The `.bober/memory/` lessons store accepts an optional per-team namespace threaded through memory.ts and retrieve.ts and all callers. The default/programming namespace resolves to the current path; named teams get a subdir, so teams' lessons never cross-contaminate.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When a lesson is appended under namespace 'teamA' and the index is loaded for 'teamA', the lesson is returned; loading the index for the programming/default namespace does NOT return it (isolation in both directions).
AC2: When the memory functions are called with no namespace (back-compat), they read and write the existing `.bober/memory/` path unchanged, and pre-existing lessons remain visible.
AC3: The `bober memory` CLI and the chat memory distill derive their namespace from the active team, defaulting to the programming/current path.
AC4: retrieveRelevantLessons honors the namespace and returns only that namespace's lessons.

features.title: Team-aware pipeline-shape selection
features.description: The pipeline engine / encoded SOP is resolved from the active team's pipelineShape, falling back to global config for the programming team. A team declaring a different shape selects a different engine — 'same engine substrate, different SOP'.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When the active team is 'programming', the selected engine is identical to selectPipelineEngine(config) today (e.g. TsPipelineEngine for engine 'ts').
AC2: When the active team declares a different pipelineShape, the corresponding engine is selected, while the existing eligibility/careful downgrade rules still apply.
AC3: runPipeline resolves the active team and uses its pipelineShape; with no team specified it resolves to 'programming' and behaves as today.

features.title: Second team as data + CLI wiring + docs (the platform proof)
features.description: A minimal non-medical example team defined purely as config, plus `--team`/team selection on `bober run` and `bober chat`, plus documentation. Proves that adding a team is data, not code.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: A minimal example team is defined purely as data (a `teams` config entry) with no new code path; loadTeam returns it with its own namespace, pipelineShape, and providers.
AC2: `bober run --team <id>` and `bober chat` team selection route the run to the named team's namespace, shape, and providers; omitting the flag uses the programming team unchanged.
AC3: Lessons produced under the example team land in its memory namespace, not the programming namespace.
AC4: Documentation explains 'adding a team is data, not code', including the `teams` config shape and the deferred file-registry path.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: All new code is ESM/NodeNext with .js import extensions, strict TS, and provider-agnostic (no @anthropic-ai/sdk or openai types outside src/providers/).
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Run typecheck and build; grep new src/teams files for SDK imports (must be none).

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: The default/programming team is byte-for-byte behavior-compatible: providers, pipeline shape, and memory path are unchanged when no team is specified.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit test asserting loadTeam(config).providers deep-equals resolveRoleProviders(config) and memoryNamespace maps to the current `.bober/memory/` path; full existing suite stays green.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Team config is validated by the existing Zod config schema (single loader, single source of truth), not an ad-hoc parser.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit test parsing a config with a `teams` section + `defaultTeam` through BoberConfigSchema.

techNotes.dataModel: New src/teams/ module: Team type ({ id, displayName, memoryNamespace, providers: RoleProviderMap, pipelineShape: PipelineEngineName, roles: Role[], guardrails?: unknown }) and loadTeam(config, teamId?). New optional config: teams: Record<string, TeamConfig> + defaultTeam: string in BoberConfigSchema (src/config/schema.ts). Per-team lessons live under .bober/memory/<namespace>/ with the programming/default namespace mapping to the existing .bober/memory/ path.
securityConsiderations: Unknown team id fails fast with a clear error (never silently falls back to programming, which would hide misconfiguration).
Namespaces are constrained to safe path segments (alphanumeric/underscore/hyphen) so a team id cannot traverse outside .bober/memory/.
techNotes.existingPatterns: Reuse: resolveRoleProviders(config):RoleProviderMap (src/config/role-providers.ts:97; RoleName union + ALL_ROLES at :10,38), selectPipelineEngine(config) / resolveEngineName (src/orchestrator/workflow/selector.ts:22,50; PipelineEngine at src/orchestrator/workflow/engine.ts:9), runPipeline + its selectPipelineEngine call (src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts:970,977), the BoberConfig Zod schema + createDefaultConfig factory (src/config/schema.ts), the loader's resolveRoleProviders call (src/config/loader.ts:264), the chat client wiring (src/cli/commands/chat.ts:28), and the memory store path helpers + public fns (src/state/memory.ts: memoryDir/lessonPath/indexPath:15-25, appendLesson:195, loadLessonIndex:241, loadLesson) + retrieveRelevantLessons (src/orchestrator/memory/retrieve.ts:79). Memory callers to update: src/cli/commands/memory.ts (appendLesson:68, loadLessonIndex:58/93, loadLesson:139), src/chat/chat-session.ts (buildMemoryDistill:41), src/orchestrator/memory/distill.ts (appendLesson). CLI registration mirrors src/cli/commands/run.ts. Research: .bober/research/20260614-chattable-team-of-agents-platform.md (sections 4-5, roadmap Phase 4).

metadata.riskNotes: Reuses the existing role/provider routing, pipeline selector, and memory store. Risk concentrated in: keeping the programming team byte-for-byte behavior-compatible (S1 regression), threading a namespace through memory.ts + all callers without breaking back-compat (S2), team-aware shape selection without disturbing eligibility/careful downgrade (S3), and proving the abstraction with a data-only second team (S4). Each is isolated to its own sprint and verified independently. Dependency note: semantic_facts namespacing is deferred to Phase 3 (table does not exist yet).
